raw_response: {"summary":"Live search suggests a remaining distinct first-pass direction: encode SDSS photometric reliability and survey-depth censoring regimes, rather than adding more color, sky, locus, auxiliary, or SED-template features.","hypotheses":[{"title":"Survey-Depth Photometric Reliability Features","summary":"Represent each object by how close its ugriz measurements are to SDSS-like faint limits, bright saturation regions, and band-specific reliability regimes.","feature_family":"photometric_reliability_depth_features","feature_strategy":"Keep raw magnitudes, redshift, alpha/delta, and one-hot spectral_type/galaxy_population, then add target-free SDSS-inspired reliability features: per-band margins to nominal ugriz faint limits such as u=22.0, g=22.2, r=22.2, i=21.3, z=20.5; binary flags for bands fainter than those limits; counts and bitmask-style summaries of over-limit blue, middle, and red bands; bright/saturation-region flags using approximate SDSS PSF caution thresholds such as u<16 and g/r/i<14.5; mid-quality flags around g/r/i/u near 19.5; min/mean/std of limit margins; and reliability-weighted versions of adjacent colors where a color is multiplied by the minimum clipped margin score of its two bands. Do not use target-derived encodings or intermediate supervised models.","baseline_model_panel":"Use the same simple balanced panel across feature-search hypotheses: a scaled linear multiclass classifier with class weights, a shallow or moderately sized random-forest/extra-trees style model with balanced class handling, and one class-weighted gradient-boosted tree implementation such as LightGBM or CatBoost with the competition-recommended balanced weighting.","model_panel_rationale":"The linear model checks whether the fixed reliability thresholds create separable additive regimes, while tree models can exploit threshold interactions between faint-band flags, colors, redshift, and the two small categorical columns without turning the hypothesis into a tuning exercise.","validation_strategy":"Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on train.csv with balanced accuracy; fit any scaler or one-hot encoder inside each fold, but the fixed SDSS threshold features can be computed deterministically for train and test because they are target-free.","materialization_hint":"Implement a compact feature builder that takes a dataframe, computes fixed per-band depth margins and regime flags, creates reliability-weighted adjacent and broad colors, one-hot encodes the two categorical columns, and evaluates the shared baseline panel with balanced class handling. Keep the feature block separately toggleable so its incremental CV effect over raw magnitudes plus simple one-hots can be logged.","expected_signal":"If useful, CV should improve especially on STAR/QSO minority recall or on rows with very faint u/z/g bands, and tree feature importance should surface over-limit counts, min depth margin, and reliability-weighted blue colors; if no signal exists, the added fixed-threshold flags should be near-zero importance and linear CV should stay flat.","novelty_confidence":"medium","risk":"This partly overlaps prior magnitude/rank and color-feature work because it still uses ugriz values, but the mechanism is different: fixed survey-reliability and censoring-regime features rather than generic colors, ranks, fluxes, sky locality, or SED fits. Synthetic competition magnitudes may not preserve real SDSS depth artifacts, so the thresholds could be weak or no-op; overly many bitmask interactions could also overfit if not kept compact.","sources":["https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.10963","https://arxiv.org/abs/0710.5801","https://arxiv.org/abs/0809.3952","https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sloan_Digital_Survey"]}]}

parsed_response.summary: Live search suggests a remaining distinct first-pass direction: encode SDSS photometric reliability and survey-depth censoring regimes, rather than adding more color, sky, locus, auxiliary, or SED-template features.

hypotheses.title: Survey-Depth Photometric Reliability Features
hypotheses.summary: Represent each object by how close its ugriz measurements are to SDSS-like faint limits, bright saturation regions, and band-specific reliability regimes.
hypotheses.feature_strategy: Keep raw magnitudes, redshift, alpha/delta, and one-hot spectral_type/galaxy_population, then add target-free SDSS-inspired reliability features: per-band margins to nominal ugriz faint limits such as u=22.0, g=22.2, r=22.2, i=21.3, z=20.5; binary flags for bands fainter than those limits; counts and bitmask-style summaries of over-limit blue, middle, and red bands; bright/saturation-region flags using approximate SDSS PSF caution thresholds such as u<16 and g/r/i<14.5; mid-quality flags around g/r/i/u near 19.5; min/mean/std of limit margins; and reliability-weighted versions of adjacent colors where a color is multiplied by the minimum clipped margin score of its two bands. Do not use target-derived encodings or intermediate supervised models.
hypotheses.baseline_model_panel: Use the same simple balanced panel across feature-search hypotheses: a scaled linear multiclass classifier with class weights, a shallow or moderately sized random-forest/extra-trees style model with balanced class handling, and one class-weighted gradient-boosted tree implementation such as LightGBM or CatBoost with the competition-recommended balanced weighting.
hypotheses.model_panel_rationale: The linear model checks whether the fixed reliability thresholds create separable additive regimes, while tree models can exploit threshold interactions between faint-band flags, colors, redshift, and the two small categorical columns without turning the hypothesis into a tuning exercise.
hypotheses.validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on train.csv with balanced accuracy; fit any scaler or one-hot encoder inside each fold, but the fixed SDSS threshold features can be computed deterministically for train and test because they are target-free.
hypotheses.materialization_hint: Implement a compact feature builder that takes a dataframe, computes fixed per-band depth margins and regime flags, creates reliability-weighted adjacent and broad colors, one-hot encodes the two categorical columns, and evaluates the shared baseline panel with balanced class handling. Keep the feature block separately toggleable so its incremental CV effect over raw magnitudes plus simple one-hots can be logged.
hypotheses.expected_signal: If useful, CV should improve especially on STAR/QSO minority recall or on rows with very faint u/z/g bands, and tree feature importance should surface over-limit counts, min depth margin, and reliability-weighted blue colors; if no signal exists, the added fixed-threshold flags should be near-zero importance and linear CV should stay flat.
hypotheses.risk: This partly overlaps prior magnitude/rank and color-feature work because it still uses ugriz values, but the mechanism is different: fixed survey-reliability and censoring-regime features rather than generic colors, ranks, fluxes, sky locality, or SED fits. Synthetic competition magnitudes may not preserve real SDSS depth artifacts, so the thresholds could be weak or no-op; overly many bitmask interactions could also overfit if not kept compact.
